title: Computing Hardware: The GPU Wars
concept: The frontier of AI is anchored on hardware: massive GPU clusters (H100/B200) and optical networking NVLinks power training scale.

exercises.prompt: What physical resource is the most critical bottleneck for training massive AI models today?

options.text: Computer chips (GPUs) and fast network connections
Just writing longer lines of code

exercises.explain: AI models learn by running millions of simple math calculations at the same time. GPUs are designed specifically to handle this parallel workload.

cards.title: The Battle of Silicon and Light
cards.body: Did you know that the race to build the smartest AI isn't just about clever code? It's actually a physical war over silicon and light. The companies with the fastest chips and the quickest connections are the ones winning the AI race today.

cards.title: What are GPUs and NVLinks?
cards.body: GPUs (like NVIDIA's H100 and B200) are specialized computer chips designed to do thousands of simple math operations at the exact same time. To train modern AI, we connect thousands of these chips using NVLink—a super-fast optical network highway that lets them share data instantly as if they were one giant super-chip.

cards.title: The Giant School Bus
cards.body: Imagine a single CPU is a luxury sports car that can deliver one VIP passenger very fast. A GPU is like a giant school bus that can carry 1,000 children at the same time. The children are the tiny math calculations, and the NVLink is a dedicated, super-fast highway lane that lets these school buses talk to each other without getting stuck in normal traffic.

cards.title: AI Supercomputers in Action
cards.body: When companies train models like ChatGPT, they don't use a single computer. They build football-field-sized data centers housing over 100,000 H100 GPUs all linked by optical cables. Without this hardware infrastructure, training a modern AI would take hundreds of years instead of a few weeks.

cards.title: Myth: AI is Just Software
cards.body: Many people believe that to make a smarter AI, you just need a better software algorithm. In reality, modern AI progress is bottlenecked by hardware. If you don't have access to thousands of connected GPUs, even the best code in the world cannot train a state-of-the-art model.

cards.title: CPU vs. GPU Cluster
cards.body: Let's compare standard computer setups with the massive hardware clusters used to train frontier AI.

right.text: Uses thousands of GPUs connected by optical NVLinks to solve millions of math equations all at once.

left.text: Uses a CPU to solve complex tasks one step at a time. Great for browsing and office work, but too slow for training big AI.

exercises.prompt: Why are GPUs (like the H100) preferred over standard CPUs for training AI models?

options.text: They run thousands of math calculations at the same time.
They are cheaper than regular computer chips.
They consume less electricity than a lightbulb.
They make internet download speeds faster.

exercises.explain: GPUs excel at parallel processing, meaning they can perform thousands of simple mathematical steps simultaneously, which is exactly how AI neural networks learn.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you have the fastest GPUs, the network cables connecting them do not matter at all.
exercises.explain: Even the fastest GPUs need high-speed connections like NVLink. Without fast networking, the chips waste time waiting for data to travel between them, causing a bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing hardware component that connects multiple GPUs at lightning speed:
exercises.sentence: While GPUs do the heavy math, the super-fast optical connection called [blank] binds them together.
exercises.explain: NVLink acts as a super-fast optical highway, allowing thousands of GPUs to work together as a single brain without slowing down.

exercises.prompt: Identify which setup represents a true AI supercomputing cluster capable of training a frontier model:

options.text: A high-end gaming laptop running on a standard CPU.
A cluster of 10,000 B200 GPUs connected by high-speed NVLink.
A warehouse of GPUs that are not connected to each other.

exercises.explain: The setup with 10,000 GPUs linked by NVLink represents a modern AI supercomputer. A single home PC or unconnected chips cannot handle the scale of training a modern frontier AI model.

exercises.prompt: If you are building the next world-class AI model, which hardware setup will give you the massive scale and speed required?

options.text: A single CPU that performs complex tasks one by one without talking to other chips
Thousands of GPUs linked by ultra-fast optical NVLink networks to process math in parallel
Large hard drives with slow cable connections to store data offline
Replacing all chips with internet modems to search the web faster

exercises.explain: AI training requires millions of math calculations running simultaneously. GPUs act like massive teams of simple calculators, and optical links like NVLink allow them to communicate instantly, preventing slow data transfers from bottlenecking the system.
